events.title: ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan
events.description: The UN’s top human rights official has issued an urgent warning that an imminent offensive against El Obeid, the capital of Sudan’s North Kordofan state, carries the risk of serious international crimes and threatens to deepen an already catastrophic humanitarian crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Stop this madness’: Rights chief warns of impending atrocities as militia closes in on El Obeid, Sudan

events.title: World Refugee Day: UN calls for renewed commitment and solidarity
events.description: The UN High Commissioner for Refugees has called on the international community to strengthen support for the nearly 42 million people worldwide who have fled their home countries to escape conflict, violence or persecution.

events.title: Pakistan issues nationwide alert over fears of heavy rains, floods
events.description: Warning comes as country braces for likely fourth consecutive year of punishing monsoon, expected to arrive this month.

events.title: Teen accused of planning terror attack on Peter Dutton and Brisbane march was making ‘edgy joke’, court told
events.description: Defence barrister claims teenager was a ‘troubled kid’ but crown prosecutor says he was serious when he wrote of copying US domestic terrorists Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A teenager accused of preparing a nail bombing campaign was either a “troubled kid” who made edgy jokes or a determined terrorist in waiting, a court has been told. The teenager, who cannot be named due to his age, spent last week on trial in the Brisbane supreme court after pleading not guilty to one count of acts done in preparation for or planning a terrorist act. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Teen accused of planning terror attack on Peter Dutton and Brisbane march was making ‘edgy joke’, court told

events.title: Confirmed Ebola cases in Congo outbreak top 1,000 with 254 deaths, authorities say

events.title: Two-train crash leaves at least 1 dead, 89 injured as emergency crews rush to chaotic scene

events.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
events.description: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the world. The officials said the outbreak, now the third largest on record, required “strong immediate support”, but that the global risk remained low. Continue reading...

events.title: From ‘media deserts’ to the invisibility of women, rights experts spotlight latest trends
events.description: From the invisibility of women and girls to “media deserts” amid an artificial intelligence (AI) tsunami, dozens of UN independent experts have been shedding light on the battle for equal rights worldwide as the Geneva-based Human Rights Council continued its 62nd session on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ From ‘media deserts’ to the invisibility of women, rights experts spotlight latest trends

events.title: Manhunt underway for Kansas City shooting spree suspect near World Cup venue; FBI offering $25K reward
events.description: The FBI is offering a $25,000 reward in the hunt for Oscar Sanchez-Munoz, 22, accused of a violent cross-state crime spree, including highway shootings near a World Cup venue.

events.title: Counterterrorism officials investigating after suspect goes on rampage in alleged hate attack: report

events.title: Former US Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan dies at 100
events.description: Alan Greenspan – the longtime US Federal Reserve chief who served under four presidents – has died at 100, his wife said in a statement on Monday. Greenspan presided over an unprecedented period of American economic expansion but was later faulted for failing to rein in markets ahead of the 2008 global financial crisis.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Former US Federal Reserve chairman Alan Greenspan dies at 100

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Gaza in the spotlight as dire conditions continue
events.description: The Security Council debated conditions in Gaza at the request of its 10 elected members amid concern that the territory's humanitarian crisis is being overshadowed by wider regional developments. The meeting took place under a ceasefire that has existed in name since October 2025 – but nearly 1,000 Palestinians have been killed, and most Gazans remain displaced. Relief chief Tom Fletcher told ambassadors that “fragile gains” since the truce are “the bare minimum of what Palestinians need.” Full coverage here, and wider Meetings Coverage can be found here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Gaza in the spotlight as dire conditions continue

events.title: Gaza: Sneeze and you might get shot, warns UNICEF in alert on child killings
events.description: After another deadly night of clashes in Lebanon, aid agencies issued a new alert for Gaza, where 265 Palestinian children have been killed since a ceasefire was announced in October 2025.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza: Sneeze and you might get shot, warns UNICEF in alert on child killings

events.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
events.description: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

In 2021, with their toddler, Alejandro, and a handful of belongings, the married couple ventured north hoping to reach safety in the US. The journey, through Guatemala and Mexico , was filled with danger and uncertainty . Continue reading...

events.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
events.description: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country. For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubble, logs and debris in protest against the conservative government. Continue reading...

events.title: French government holds crisis meeting over sweltering heatwave
events.description: France is set to experience the hottest day yet in its latest heatwave after a sweltering night, with a peak of 43C expected in the southwest. Forecasters warn the extreme weather episode could end up being as serious as a 2003 heatwave that claimed the lives of nearly 15,000 people nationwide. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar takes a look at the French government's recommendations as it holds its latest crisis meeting.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 French government holds crisis meeting over sweltering heatwave

events.title: Climate shocks accelerating as El Niño threat looms over already vulnerable regions

events.title: For Iraqi minorities, World Cup representation is a source of pride
events.description: France faces Iraq in a group stage match on June 22. The Iraqi team faces a significant disadvantage in this challenging group. But even after a 4-1 loss to Norway in its first match, for the country, this World Cup appearance remains an event not to be missed following a 40 year absence from the competition. This is especially true in Kurdistan in the north of the country where the presence of players from national minorities is significant.

events.title: AI models that can take down governments and business months away, rare Five Eyes statement warns
events.description: Signal agencies in Australia, the US, the UK, New Zealand and Canada sound alarm after Trump blocks foreign nationals from Anthropic’s Fable AI model Powerful AI models capable of taking down governments and businesses are mere months away, cyber intelligence agencies for the Five Eyes have warned in a rare joint statement, urging leaders to “act now”. The surprising public intervention by signals agencies for Australia, the US, the UK, New Zealand and Canada comes after the Trump administration earlier this month decided to block “foreign nationals” from using a much-hyped AI model built by tech company Anthropic, called Fable. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 AI models that can take down governments and business months away, rare Five Eyes statement warns

events.title: Ghana conference calls for formal apology for transatlantic slave trade
events.description: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at event includes demand for compensation and debt relief More than money: the logic of slavery reparations A global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade. Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

events.title: Cape Verde fan goes wild live on BBC News as his country scores

events.title: French heatwave drives up demand for AC, despite concerns
events.description: The second early-season heatwave of the year has brought the return of a politically-charged issue. That of air-conditioning - for decades, France has been notoriously reticent to adopt the technology - currently just 25% of French households have it. That figure is up significantly from just a decade ago, and it's set to keep rising as climate change brings increasingly frequent episodes of extreme heat. France's largely nuclear-based power generation means that the increased electricity consumption of AC has less of an environmental impact than elsewhere - but there are other concerns.

events.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
events.description: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with 23mm reported in Kranj. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia

events.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
events.description: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majority A Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” has defeated a leftwing senator by a razor-thin margin to win Colombia’s presidential runoff, in an election which promises to mark a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict. With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, the far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than the leftist senator Iván Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were cast blank. Continue reading...

events.title: Colombians vote in runoff election expected to trigger shift in decades-long armed conflict
events.description: Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote Colombians are going to the polls in a presidential runoff expected to trigger a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Polls show the frontrunner is the Trump-admiring far-right lawyer and millionaire businessman Abelardo de la Espriella, who has vowed to abandon President Gustavo Petro’s “total peace” plan of negotiating the disarmament of all criminal organisations and instead return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups. Continue reading...

events.title: Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote
events.description: Colombians will choose on Sunday between two men whose lives have been very differently shaped by the militias, and whose visions for the country are poles apart Whoever wins Sunday’s presidential runoff vote in Colombia, the country’s next leader will have a personal history intertwined with one of the criminal forces at the heart of a decades-long armed conflict that claimed nearly half a million lives. The lives of Iván Cepeda and Abelardo de la Espriella have, in very different ways, been shaped by their relationship with Colombia’s paramilitaries – private armies originally established by rightwing landowners, drug traffickers, businessmen, mining magnates and politicians to fight leftwing guerrilla groups. Continue reading...

events.title: 'JD Vance put lipstick on a pig: Iranians have the upper hand ' in peace negotiations
events.description: Annette Young is pleased to welcome Scott Lucas, Professor of American Studies and Int'l Politics at the Clinton Institute, University College Dublin. According to Lucas, the US is not negotiating with Iran from a position of strength: "The Americans shifted from regime change to regime surrender", he explains, "trying to bomb their way to get the Iranians to capitulate on a number of conditions. That failed." So as the US ruled out ground troops, they had to come "back to the table". And Lucas said the US really miscalculated with respect to: Iran's retaliation on the Gulf states and the impact the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz would have on the global economy.
events.ticker_label: 💥 'JD Vance put lipstick on a pig: Iranians have the upper hand ' in peace negotiations

events.description: The US cited progress in talks with Tehran on allowing nuclear inspections and opening the Strait of Hormuz in temporarily lifting oil sanctions. JD Vance said a "foundation" is in place for a final deal. More on DW.

events.title: Maternity leave in India offers progress, with mixed results
events.description: In 2025, the Supreme Court of India declared maternity leave a fundamental right for working women. India is among the countries in the world where pregnant women are entitled to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave, which can be taken for up to two children. The move has been widely welcomed by women's rights advocates. But they emphasise that India still has a long way to go in ensuring the rights of working mothers. FRANCE 24's Navodita Kumari and Zubair Dar report.

events.title: 'Doomchessing' is the new digital addiction, as users flock to Chess.com

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
events.description: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

events.title: From camel coats to guochao: Max Mara woos China’s luxury brand consumers
events.description: Fashion house pays tribute to Chinese style with its 75th anniversary catwalk show in Shanghai “New York may be the city that never sleeps, but Shanghai doesn’t even sit down.” For the British designer Ian Griffiths, who encountered this line in the New Yorker, it summed up why China’s biggest city was the right place to celebrate Max Mara’s 75th anniversary. “Max Mara is a product for metropolitan women, and it would be patronising to assume that a metropolitan wardrobe should be western-centric,” Griffiths said. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From camel coats to guochao: Max Mara woos China’s luxury brand consumers

events.title: Almost three tonnes of cocaine found buried under Sydney property in Australia’s biggest ever seizure, police say
events.description: Australian federal police arrested and charged two men after allegedly finding $800m worth of cocaine in ‘bunkers’ under shipping containers Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Police claim to have made Australia’s biggest ever cocaine bust after finding $800m worth of the drug buried under false flooring on a semi-rural property. Two men, aged 21 and 25, allegedly tried to run from police and were arrested on Friday after an operation by Australian federal police, alongside investigators in multiple states. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Almost three tonnes of cocaine found buried under Sydney property in Australia’s biggest ever seizure, police say

events.title: 75 Years of the Goethe-Institut: Germany's global cultural voice
events.description: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Cape Verde stun again, Spain get their redemption
events.description: Spain got their World Cup redemption and their first win by comfortably beating Saudi Arabia 4-0. Belgium went down to 10 men after the eighth red card of the tournament was handed out. The Red Devils struggled against Iran, and that game ended on a dry tally of 0-0. The surprise of the evening played out in Miami, as Cape Verde managed to draw against Uruguay. Egypt finally got their hands on their first World Cup win as the Pharaohs won 3-1 against New Zealand. Plus, France get ready for their game against Iraq this Monday.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Norway’s ‘Viking row’ takes over Times Square
events.description: Norway fans took their signature ‘Viking row’ to New York’s Times Square on Sunday on the eve of their World Cup clash with Senegal. Since it was performed in Boston ahead of Norway’s World Cup opener last week, the “row” has gone viral and has been seen on escalators, boats and even in the Norwegian parliament.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Yamal boosts Spain as Belgium held to draw again
events.description: After a disappointing opening draw against Cape Verde, Spain comfortably brushed aside Saudi Arabia (4-0), powered by Lamine Yamal's first-ever World Cup goal and an Oyarzabal brace.

events.title: UNESCO launches consultation on fair payment for news in the digital age
events.description: UN cultural agency UNESCO has launched a global consultation process to inform its Draft Guidance on Fair Compensation for News, particularly as online platforms and artificial intelligence (AI) increasingly rely on journalistic content.

events.title: Old and new challenges for the Human Rights Council as it turns 20
events.description: It’s 20 years to the day since the UN Human Rights Council began its work as the world’s principal forum tasked with promoting and defending fundamental rights everywhere, particularly the world’s most vulnerable people.

events.title: UN upholds freedom of movement for peacekeepers in Lebanon
events.description: The United Nations has again called for freedom of movement for its peacekeepers in Lebanon who continue to closely monitor developments in the south of the country, including in the wake of the recent provisional agreement signed by the United States and Iran.

events.title: EXCLUSIVE: Meet the man Israel chose to be its first-ever ambassador to the Christian world
events.description: Ambassador George Deek explains the mission behind his new role as special envoy to the Christian world and Israel's efforts to strengthen the Judeo-Christian bond.

events.title: The ICC must investigate Israel’s genocidal use of sexual violence
events.description: There is a growing body of evidence of the systematic nature of sexual assaults against Palestinian detainees.

events.title: Why Coca-Cola and the US taxman are at war over a $20bn tax bill
events.description: Beverage giant is appealing a 2020 ruling that has major implications for how much tax firms pay on overseas profits.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why Coca-Cola and the US taxman are at war over a $20bn tax bill

events.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
events.description: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

events.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
events.description: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been given no choice. They thought it would be a temporary evacuation after Indonesia had won independence. Continue reading...

events.title: Iran-US talks to end war beset by tension, mistrust
events.description: While both sides claim "encouraging progress," experts say there's a long way to go to turn the shaky diplomatic framework into a durable agreement.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran-US talks to end war beset by tension, mistrust

events.title: What do Iranians think of the Iran-US deal?
events.description: As talks continue between the US and Iran, our colleagues at France 2 got access to rare on-the-ground perspectives in Tehran. Anti-regime residents had hoped that the war would bring change. But with Iranian leaders seemingly more unified than ever, their disappointment is hitting hard.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What do Iranians think of the Iran-US deal?

events.title: Replay: JD Vance addresses US-Iran talks
events.description: Rewatch as JD Vance addresses talks between US and Iran in Switzerland to put an end to the war in the Middle East.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Replay: JD Vance addresses US-Iran talks

events.title: Trump-Meloni row intensifies as leaders trade barbs
events.description: Italian prime minister Giorgia Meloni has called out US President Donald Trump for "senseless", "constant, unprovoked attacks", as a row triggered by his claim she begged him for a picture escalates.

events.title: Pope Leo XIV warns wars are 'fed' faster than people as aid money dries up
events.description: Pope Leo XIV says wars are being sustained more easily than people are fed
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo XIV warns wars are 'fed' faster than people as aid money dries up

events.title: France sizzles in a week of punishing heat that is already causing deaths
events.description: France is facing a grueling heat wave with temperatures soaring above 40 degrees Celsius, or 104 Fahrenheit

events.title: Taiwan begins 5-day military drill with tanks patrolling streets
events.description: Taiwan has launched a five-day set of military drills aimed at boosting combat readiness against potential Chinese attacks

events.title: Vance says talks with Iranian officials set 'good foundation' for a deal to end the war

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Vance says talks with Iranian officials set 'good foundation' for a deal to end the war

events.title: Vigil planned for 18-year-old tourist killed in Central Park horse-drawn carriage incident
events.description: A vigil will honor Romanch Mahajan, the 18-year-old Indian tourist who died after falling from a horse-drawn carriage in Central Park last week.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Vigil planned for 18-year-old tourist killed in Central Park horse-drawn carriage incident

events.title: Concertgoer dies after apparent fall from upper level at Madison Square Garden
events.description: A 51-year-old man suffered a fatal fall from the upper levels of Madison Square Garden during a sold-out Goose concert on Saturday, police said.

events.title: South Carolina fitness trainer told friends she wanted to leave her now-husband years before body was found
events.description: Friends say missing South Carolina trainer Elena Katherine Moore wanted to leave her now-husband before her body was discovered this week.

events.title: Keir Starmer reportedly considering stepping down as PM and could announce timetable for departure
events.description: British PM Keir Starmer is reportedly considering stepping down, with senior Labor figures expecting a statement on his future as early as next week.

events.title: Israeli ambassador, UN official shout over each other in tense exchange over sexual violence blacklist
events.description: Israeli Ambassador Danny Danon erupted at a U.N. official during a tense heated exchange over Israel's placement on a U.N. sexual violence blacklist.

events.title: Trump's $300B Iran investment fund may be 'close to impossible' due to IRGC sanctions law, expert warns
events.description: Trump's $300B Iran investment fund faces legal roadblocks as expert warns IRGC-controlled construction sector creates sanctions risks under existing law.

events.title: Guinea bans exports of raw gold to boost local refining
events.description: The prohibition is intended to boost the country's economy by refining gold domestically.

events.title: Jonno Duniam calls Pauline Hanson’s push for monoculture ‘bizarre’ – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Western Australian poultry farms locked down as second wild bird dies from H5N1 bird flu Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Hume has no problem with more debate on NDIS changes, but criticises ‘horse trading’ between Labor and the Greens Jane Hume is speaking to RN Breakfast now, saying the Coalition has no problem with increased scrutiny over changes to the NDIS should the Greens negotiate a longer inquiry into the scheme the week. I suppose one of our concerns is that perhaps the changes don’t go far enough in addressing the rorts and the criminal behaviour that we’ve seen embedded in the NDIS system now. We want to make sure that they are appropriately addressed. And we also want to make sure that Australia’s most vulnerable are well cared for. And there does seem to be some oversights in there. So I have no problems with scrutiny of legislation. It’s no surprise that the Greens have got into horse trading with Labor over this. We know exactly what it is that they do. This is their modus operandi. I think that speaks volumes of this Labor government’s priorities. Continue reading...

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Ukrainian soldiers march in Kyiv's Pride parade to push for equal rights
events.description: Ukrainian soldiers and their partners march in Kyiv's Pride parade for the right to marry and enter into civil partnerships in a traditional country that's trying to modernize as it aligns with Western Europe.

events.title: Colombia Election: Right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella wins, initial count shows
events.description: Colombia wakes up to a sharp political turn to right as Abelardo de la Espriella's preliminary victory redraws the country's path on security, economy, and peace.

events.title: Who is Andy Burnham's, the UK's likely next prime minister?
events.description: Britain's prime minister is expected to lay out a timetable for his departure. Keir Starmer has been under pressure to go since last month's dismal election outcome for the Labour Party. The man vying the country's top job is the Mayor of Greater Manchester, who's just secured his return to parliament. But who exactly is Andy Burnham?

events.title: Alan Greenspan, longtime head of the US Federal Reserve, dies aged 100
events.description: Alan Greenspan, the longtime head of the Federal Reserve and influential economist, has died aged 100.

events.title: Israel kills turtle conservationist Mona Khalil

events.title: Why is Bulgaria threatening to veto EU's Russia sanctions?
events.description: Bulgarian PM Rumen Radev has vowed to veto the EU's latest sanctions against Russia because they include Russian Orthodox Patriarch Kirill and former Lukoil president Vagit Alekperov. What's behind his opposition?

events.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
events.description: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

events.title: Europe under red alert amid exceptional early heatwave
events.description: It's been less than a month since a May heatwave set records across several European countries. Now the heat is back and temperatures across the continent may raise even higher in coming days. According to the WHO, 200,000 people across the EU have died due to heat since 2022.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Europe under red alert amid exceptional early heatwave

events.title: Fire tears through building in northern India, killing 14, mostly students
events.description: A fire has torn through a commercial building in Lucknow, India, killing at least 14 people, mostly students

events.title: Ebola in DR Congo: One month on, scaled up response remains insufficient
events.description: One month after the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak was declared in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, case numbers continue to rise.

events.title: Responders put out explosive blaze at fireworks stand in Oklahoma
events.description: Broken Arrow Fire Department in Oklahoma responded to a fireworks stand blaze "with fireworks actively detonating," swiftly quenching the flames.

events.title: US military conducts strike on another vessel carrying alleged narco-traffickers, killing 2

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: US-Iran talks end in Switzerland, mediators say
events.description: According to mediators from Qatar and Pakistan, the talks between United States and Iranian officials in Switzerland have ended with both sides agreeing on a roadmap. Technical talks will continue.

events.title: US makes $1 billion contribution to UN child rights and food agencies
events.description: Two United Nations agencies have together welcomed more than $1 billion in assistance from the United States to support their operations targeting millions of children and hungry families in more than 40 countries.

events.title: Supreme Court reinstates conviction in 1979 Etan Patz murder
events.description: No description available.

events.title: Ex-Wimbledon champion Vondrousova banned for refusing drug test
events.description: Marketa Vondrousova, who won Wimbledon in 2023, was issued a four year suspension after she refused to submit a ⁠sample.

events.title: Qatar PM denies snubbing US vice president in viral video

events.title: JD Vance touts progress on key issues in US-Iran negotiations
events.description: JD Vance touts progress on key issues in US-Iran negotiations.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: Ex-Olympian denies vandalising Trump's $14m reflective pool project
events.description: Donald Trump has claimed vandals are responsible for issues with the Reflecting Pool in Washington DC following a refurbishment costing more than $14m (£10.5m).

events.title: Art and culture can both fuel and counter hate, UN discussion hears on Juneteenth
events.description: From a gold collar worn by an enslaved African to music preserved for nearly a century after the Holocaust, a staged reconstruction of a hate radio broadcast or porcelain coffee cups laid out in participatory remembrance, artists in a United Nations discussion on Friday used powerful images to underscore how culture can shape the fight against hatred.

events.title: Trump says UK's Starmer will resign, wishes him well amid reports of PM's political future
events.description: U.S. President Donald Trump said United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer will step down, wishing the foreign leader well, while also leveling criticism.

events.title: China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan
events.description: Taiwan's agriculture ministry is worried that Beijing wants to weaponise a local specialty: the atemoya.
events.ticker_label: 💥 China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: 'Ballet brings me so much joy and I want to spread it': Kadeem En Pointe
events.description: A male ballet dancer is on a mission to encourage more male dancers to take up the more traditional female role of one who goes up on his or her toes, or en pointe. Kadeem En Pointe has filmed himself for the first time here in Paris dancing in front of the city's major monuments. He spoke to us about his ongoing campaign in Perspective.

events.title: Keir Starmer resigns as British prime minister after devastating Labour revolt and local election losses
events.description: British PM Keir Starmer announced his resignation after Labour's devastating local election losses and a mounting revolt from within his own party ranks.

events.title: First round of US-Iran talks ends with encouraging progress, mediators say
events.description: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

events.title: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
events.description: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

events.title: Largest ever cocaine bust in Australia after police raid underground bunker
events.description: Police seized 2.7 tonnes of cocaine worth an estimated A$816m after searching a property in western Sydney.

events.title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
events.description: Martha Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.

events.title: Japan quintuples visa fees in first price hike since 1978

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
events.description: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countries Barbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement. Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...

events.title: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
events.description: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese culture The activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar. The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a section of the wall near Beijing. Continue reading...

events.title: UK PM Keir Starmer resigns. And, US and Iran agree to roadmap for final deal
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday. And, the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a "roadmap" to reach a final deal within 60 days.

events.title: Keir Starmer announces resignation as UK prime minister
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Monday he is stepping down as leader of the governing Labour Party and will leave office within weeks, scarcely two years after being elected in a landslide.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Keir Starmer announces resignation as UK prime minister

events.title: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
events.description: The U.K. will have its seventh prime minister in 10 years. Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor is the outgoing Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham.

events.title: Australian police uncover 3 tons of cocaine
events.description: The drug was found on June 19 in plastic tubs buried in bunkers hidden beneath three shipping containers. Two Sydney residents were arrested. They face potential sentences of life in prison.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Australian police uncover 3 tons of cocaine

events.title: China hits back at U.S. sanctions restricting its exports to American defense firms
events.description: China on Monday announced sanctions on 10 American military-related companies in response to a recent U.S. move that bars some leading Chinese tech companies from defense contracts.

events.title: Germany and UK rebuilding ties 10 years after Brexit vote
events.description: Ten years after the referendum, the two countries are strengthening their ties once again. The stability of these ties is now being put to the test with Keir Starmer to be replaced as prime minister.

events.title: How inflation undermines Nigeria's sugar tax
events.description: Nigeria's sugary drinks tax was meant to stop rising diabetes rates, but soaring inflation has eroded its impact. Experts say the levy is too small to change behavior and urge higher rates and reforms.

events.title: India news: NEET retest saw millions of students turn up
events.description: More than 2 million students appeared for the high-stake NEET medical entrance exam after the question paper leak fiasco amid high security. Follow DW for more.

events.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
events.description: In an emotional speech at 10 Downing Street, Keir Starmer said he will step down after days of mounting pressure and speculation over his future. It comes as leadership contender Andy Burnham returns to parliament.

events.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
events.description: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

events.title: Germany news: Could heatwave temperatures hit 40°C?
events.description: Germany is in the grip of an intense heatwave, with experts predicting extreme temperatures in some regions. Chancellor Friedrich Merz insists he can handle the heat and is not bothered by personal insults. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Germany news: Could heatwave temperatures hit 40°C?

events.title: Colombia election: Hard-right candidate claims victory
events.description: Right-wing presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, won 49.66% of the Colombians' votes, according to preliminary results.

events.title: Czechia: Thousands march in support of public media funding
events.description: Prime Minister Andrej Babis' government has been accused of seeking to take control over Czech public media. The plan would leave broadcasters with a 15% funding cut and no guarantees of continued future funding.

events.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
events.description: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

events.title: Change of UK PM likely 'a passing of the crown' while early general election unlikely
events.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was stepping down as leader of the governing Labour Party but would remain caretaker prime minister until a new head is chosen by the party. His likely successor is Andy Burnham, who won the Makerfield byelection a few days earlier. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle looks at the likely scenarios for the UK.

events.title: Anatomy of a fall: Keir Starmer, from election landslide to precipitous descent
events.description: Dutiful rather than dynamic, Keir Starmer was elected to be a safe pair of hands after years of chaos under Britain’s Conservatives. His term ended less than two years later amid corruption allegations, policy U-turns, and after links were revealed between his choice for US ambassador and Jeffrey Epstein, a man Starmer never met.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Anatomy of a fall: Keir Starmer, from election landslide to precipitous descent

events.title: Northern Ireland's former unionist leader convicted of decades-old child sex abuse claims
events.description: Jeffrey Donaldson, former leader of Northern Ireland’s largest unionist party, has been convicted of rape and sex abuse charges involving two girls

events.title: How Keir Starmer will be replaced as UK prime minister and who could succeed him
events.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has conceded that he has lost the support of his party members in Parliament and will step down as soon as his Labour Party chooses a successor, possibly as soon as next month

events.title: A German journalist was released from Syrian prison after months and is back in Berlin
events.description: A German journalist detained in Syria has been released

events.title: Germany plans to take 40% in Leopard tank maker KNDS, joining France as stakeholder
events.description: The German government says it intends to take a 40% stake in defense contractor KNDS, whose products include Leopard and Leclerc tanks, as it tries to strengthen European production along with NATO ally France

events.title: Starmer went from election landslide to downfall after his supporters deserted him
events.description: Keir Starmer was elected Britain’s prime minister in 2024 to be a safe pair of hands who would end years of political chaos under the Conservatives
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Starmer went from election landslide to downfall after his supporters deserted him

events.title: Former S. Korean minister gets 25-year prison term for role in martial law imposition
events.description: A former South Korean justice minister has been sentenced to 25 years in prison

events.title: Former East Timor president and independence fighter Francisco Guterres dies at 71
events.description: Francisco Guterres, former president of East Timor and a key figure in its independence movement, has died at 71
events.ticker_label: 💥 Former East Timor president and independence fighter Francisco Guterres dies at 71

events.title: Australia and Canada sign a $1.75B deal to build long-range radar in Canada
events.description: Australia and Canada have signed a $1.75 billion export agreement to build an Australian-designed long-range radar system in Canada

events.title: Britain's economic woes fuel Brexit discontent a decade after vote to leave EU
events.description: Simon Boyd’s firm makes prefabricated steel structures on the south coast of England and ships them to customers as far away as Ghana and Barbados

events.title: For one young refugee in Uganda, basketball is more than a game
events.description: When 19-year-old Stephane Kulimushi looks around the basketball court in Kampala where he trains young refugees, he sees more than players.

events.title: Fermentation, flavour and the future of food: Making sustainability delicious
events.description: Words like “sustainability” and “healthy” shouldn’t take the joy out of eating. As elite chef Jaume Biarnés has been explaining to UN News, sustainable gastronomy can be delicious, exciting and fun.

events.title: Keir Starmer resigns as British prime minister and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: NYPD cop shoots suspect attempting to flee in stolen vehicle, officials say
events.description: An NYPD officer shot a driver who allegedly struck multiple cops while trying to flee in a stolen Kia Sportage in the Bronx, authorities say.

events.title: Georgia pair charged with murder after bartender's dismembered remains found in lake outside Atlanta
events.description: A Georgia pair allegedly murdered a bartender and dumped his dismembered remains in a lake outside Atlanta, according to investigators.

events.title: Multiple hikers dead amid scorching Grand Canyon temperatures
events.description: Several individuals have passed away this month apparently in connection with the blazing hot temperatures that are impacting the Grand Canyon.

events.title: MMA fighter helps nab Florida university student leader in suspected child predator sting
events.description: MMA fighter Dustin Lampros and 561 Predator Catchers allegedly caught a Florida college student who is accused of planning to meet a 13-year-old boy at Home Depot.

events.title: Luigi Mangione overruled his own lawyers with a 'change of heart' on psych defense, legal expert says
events.description: Luigi Mangione withdrew his proposed psychiatric defense in the UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson murder case hours before a key court deadline.

events.title: FBI captures $1.2B Medicare fraud fugitive in Philippines, second arrest from Most Wanted Fraudsters list
events.description: Herbert Leon Kimble, accused of a $1.2 billion Medicare fraud scheme targeting the elderly, was captured in the Philippines and returned to the U.S.

events.title: Meloni's spat with Trump is calculated strategy to boost her approval ratings: expert
events.description: Analyst says Meloni's public feud with President Trump is a calculated strategy to boost her declining approval ratings ahead of Italy's 2027 general election.

events.title: Iran's unprecedented 'whole-regime' delegation at US deal talks signals one goal: expert
events.description: Iran sent a whole-regime delegation, including its central bank governor and oil officials to Switzerland talks, signaling demands for immediate cash flow.

events.title: Trump-backed 'El Tigre' looks to crush cartels, end Colombia's socialist era in pivotal election
events.description: Abelardo de la Espriella, known as El Tigre, rides voter frustration over crime and cartels in Colombia's high-stakes presidential runoff election.

events.title: African and Caribbean leaders call for payments, debt cancellation, formal apologies over slavery
events.description: African and Caribbean leaders adopted a 19-point reparations plan in Ghana demanding financial compensation, debt cancellation and formal apologies.

events.title: H5N1 bird flu confirmed in Australia for the first time, meaning virus has now reached every continent
events.description: Australia confirms its first case of H5N1 bird flu in a seabird near Esperance, Western Australia, meaning the virus has now reached every continent.

events.title: 'Send them back' chants rock European Parliament after landslide vote to speed up deportations
events.description: European Parliament approved the Return Regulation 418-218, sparking chants of "send them back" from right-wing lawmakers in a deeply divided vote.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 'Send them back' chants rock European Parliament after landslide vote to speed up deportations

events.title: Man charged with attempted murder, released after allegedly forcing toddler into crocodile enclosure at zoo
events.description: A man was charged with attempted murder after allegedly forcing a 3-year-old boy into a crocodile enclosure at a zoo in Huntingdon, England.

events.title: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces potential leadership challenge from newly-elected Andy Burnham
events.description: Labour's Andy Burnham won a special election on Friday and gained a seat in Parliament. He has openly spoken about challenging Keir Starmer as leader.
